title: Infobox Positioning Comparison
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: High School Geometry
description: Interactive comparison that lets learners switch infobox placement (below, side panel, floating tooltip) and hover/click triggers to judge which pairing fits a given diagram.

learningObjectives: Compare below, side panel, and floating tooltip infobox positions and determine which is most appropriate for a diagram layout.
Evaluate whether hover or click events create clearer reading sequences for dense diagrams.
prerequisites: Familiarity with UI terminology such as tooltip and side panel

keyQuestions: Which infobox placement keeps the diagram legible for your current project?
When does hover behavior overwhelm readers compared to click-to-reveal?
commonMisconceptions: Assuming a floating tooltip always saves space without covering critical regions.
Believing hover interactions work equally well on touch devices.
assessmentOpportunities: Ask students to justify their recommended placement/trigger pairing for a new diagram.
Have learners screenshot the mode they chose and annotate why it works.
